---
title: "COLORECTAL WCGNA"
format: html
editor: visual
---

**Unified colorectal normal reference from GTEx + TCGA \# All data sourced through recount3 for uniform processing**

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# complete_pipeline.R
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Strategy 1: Unified colorectal normal reference from GTEx + TCGA
# All data sourced through recount3 for uniform processing
#
# Data acquired:
#   Normal reference:
#     GTEx Colon - Sigmoid        (~320 samples)
#     GTEx Colon - Transverse     (~280 samples)
#     TCGA-COAD matched normals   (~40  samples)
#     TCGA-READ matched normals   (~10  samples)
#   Tumour validation:
#     TCGA-COAD primary tumours   (~460 samples)
#     TCGA-READ primary tumours   (~166 samples)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Install packages (run once) ───────────────────────────────────────
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

pkgs <- c("recount3", "DESeq2", "sva", "WGCNA", "flashClust",
          "edgeR", "limma", "gridExtra", "biomaRt",
          "SummarizedExperiment", "BiocParallel")

for (pkg in pkgs) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE))
        BiocManager::install(pkg)
}

# Create output directories
dir.create("data/raw",       recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("data/processed", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("data/wgcna",     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("plots",          showWarnings = FALSE)

cat("Environment ready\n")
cat("=" , rep("=", 59), "\n", sep="")
```

```{r}
# ── Load libraries ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(ggplot2)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(recount3)
    library(DESeq2)
    library(sva)
    library(WGCNA)
    library(flashClust)
    library(SummarizedExperiment)
    library(gridExtra)
})


# Allow parallel processing for WGCNA
allowWGCNAThreads()
```

# **DATA ACQUISITION via recount3**

```{r}
cat("\nBLOCK 1: Data Acquisition\n")
cat("-", rep("-", 40), "\n", sep="")

# ── Set recount3 URL — try AWS S3 first, fall back to alternatives ────
# This bypasses the duffel load balancer which causes the homes_index error

set_recount3_url <- function() {
    urls <- c(
        "https://recount-opendata.s3.amazonaws.com/recount3/release",
        "https://sciserver.org/public-data/recount3/data",
        "http://duffel.rail.bio/recount3"
    )
    for (url in urls) {
        cat("Trying recount3 URL:", url, "... ")
        tryCatch({
            options(recount3_url = url)
            test <- available_projects()
            cat("OK ✓\n")
            cat("Using:", url, "\n")
            return(TRUE)
        }, error = function(e) {
            cat("FAILED\n")
        })
    }
    stop("All recount3 URLs failed. Check your internet connection.")
}

set_recount3_url()
```

```{r}
# ── 1a. Get all human projects ────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nFetching all available human projects...\n")

human_projects <- available_projects()

cat("Total projects:", nrow(human_projects), "\n")
cat("File source breakdown:\n")
print(table(human_projects$file_source))
# Should show: gtex  sra  tcga

# Separate by source
gtex_projects <- subset(human_projects, file_source == "gtex")
tcga_projects <- subset(human_projects, file_source == "tcga")

cat("\nAll GTEx tissue projects:\n")
print(gtex_projects[, c("project", "n_samples")])

cat("\nAll TCGA projects:\n")
print(tcga_projects[, c("project", "n_samples")])
```

```{r}
# ── 1b. Identify colon GTEx tissues ──────────────────────────────────
cat("\nColon-related GTEx projects:\n")
colon_gtex <- gtex_projects[
    grepl("colon|sigmoid|transverse",
          gtex_projects$project,
          ignore.case = TRUE), ]
print(colon_gtex[, c("project", "n_samples")])

# Confirm the exact project names from the printed output
# before running the downloads below

```

```{r}
# ── 1c. Download GTEx Colon ──────────────────────────────────
cat("\nDownloading GTEx Colon...\n")

gtex_colon_info <- subset(human_projects,
    project     == "COLON" &
    file_source == "gtex"
)
if (nrow(gtex_colon_info) == 0)
    stop("COLON not found — check project names printed above")

rse_colon    <- create_rse(gtex_colon_info)
counts_colon <- assay(rse_colon, "raw_counts")
meta_colon   <- colData(rse_colon)
cat("  Samples:", ncol(counts_colon), "\n")
cat("  Genes:  ", nrow(counts_colon), "\n")
```

```{r}
# ── 1e. Download TCGA-COAD ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\nDownloading TCGA-COAD...\n")

# CRITICAL: must include project_type == "data_sources"
coad_info <- subset(human_projects,
    project      == "COAD"          &
    file_source  == "tcga"          &
    project_type == "data_sources"
)

cat("COAD project rows found:", nrow(coad_info), "\n")
if (nrow(coad_info) == 0)
    stop("TCGA-COAD not found — check available TCGA projects")

rse_coad    <- create_rse(coad_info)
counts_coad <- assay(rse_coad, "raw_counts")
meta_coad   <- colData(rse_coad)

# ── CRITICAL FIX: recount3 TCGA uses GDC UUIDs as column names ────────────────
# NOT the familiar TCGA barcodes (TCGA-XX-XXXX-01A-...)
# The TCGA barcode is stored in the metadata column: tcga.tcga_barcode
# We extract the sample type code from THAT column instead

cat("\nDiagnostic — checking column name format:\n")
cat("  First colname:", colnames(counts_coad)[1], "\n")
# You will see a UUID like: 43e715bf-28d9-4b5e-b762-8cd1b69a430e

# Check the metadata column containing TCGA barcodes
cat("  TCGA barcode example:", meta_coad$tcga.tcga_barcode[1], "\n")
# You will see: TCGA-AA-3521-01A-01R-0821-07

# Extract sample type from the TCGA barcode in metadata
# Characters 14-15 of the TCGA barcode encode sample type:
#   "01" = Primary Tumour
#   "11" = Solid Tissue Normal
tcga_barcodes_coad <- meta_coad$tcga.tcga_barcode
sample_type_coad   <- substr(tcga_barcodes_coad, 14, 15)

cat("  Sample type distribution:\n")
print(table(sample_type_coad))

# Now subset counts matrix using the sample type from metadata
# The sample order in meta_coad matches the column order in counts_coad
tumour_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, sample_type_coad == "01"]
normal_coad_counts <- counts_coad[, sample_type_coad == "11"]

cat("  TCGA-COAD tumour samples:", ncol(tumour_coad_counts), "\n")
cat("  TCGA-COAD normal samples:", ncol(normal_coad_counts), "\n")
# Expected: ~503 tumour, ~41 normal
```

```{r}
# ── 1f. Download TCGA-READ ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\nDownloading TCGA-READ...\n")

read_info <- subset(human_projects,
    project      == "READ"          &
    file_source  == "tcga"          &
    project_type == "data_sources"
)

cat("READ project rows found:", nrow(read_info), "\n")
if (nrow(read_info) == 0)
    stop("TCGA-READ not found — check available TCGA projects")

rse_read    <- create_rse(read_info)
counts_read <- assay(rse_read, "raw_counts")
meta_read   <- colData(rse_read)

# Same fix for READ
tcga_barcodes_read <- meta_read$tcga.tcga_barcode
sample_type_read   <- substr(tcga_barcodes_read, 14, 15)

cat("  Sample type distribution:\n")
print(table(sample_type_read))

tumour_read_counts <- counts_read[, sample_type_read == "01"]
normal_read_counts <- counts_read[, sample_type_read == "11"]

cat("  TCGA-READ tumour samples:", ncol(tumour_read_counts), "\n")
cat("  TCGA-READ normal samples:", ncol(normal_read_counts), "\n")
# Expected: ~166 tumour, ~10 normal
```

```{r}
# Check available TCGA barcode columns
tcga_cols <- grep("barcode|tcga_barcode",
                   colnames(meta_coad),
                   ignore.case = TRUE,
                   value = TRUE)
cat("Available barcode columns:", tcga_cols, "\n")

# If the column name differs from "tcga.tcga_barcode",
# update the meta_coad$tcga.tcga_barcode references above
# to use the actual column name printed here
```

```{r}
# ── 1g. Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nData Acquisition Summary:\n")
cat("  GTEx colon (normal):    ", ncol(counts_colon),    "\n")
cat("  TCGA-COAD normal:         ", ncol(normal_coad_counts),"\n")
cat("  TCGA-READ normal:         ", ncol(normal_read_counts),"\n")
cat("  Total normal reference:   ",
    ncol(counts_colon) +
    ncol(normal_coad_counts) + ncol(normal_read_counts), "\n")
cat("\n  TCGA-COAD tumour:         ", ncol(tumour_coad_counts), "\n")
cat("  TCGA-READ tumour:         ", ncol(tumour_read_counts), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Save immediately — protects against R session crashes during long downloads
save(counts_colon,
     normal_coad_counts, normal_read_counts,
     tumour_coad_counts, tumour_read_counts,
     meta_coad, meta_read, meta_colon, 
     file = "~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/all_raw_counts.RData")

cat("\nAll raw data saved to ~/Desktop/COLORECTAL CANCER/LCP1-NET/all_raw_counts.RData\n")
cat("Block 1 complete ✓\n")

```

**BLOCK 2 — GENE ID HARMONISATION**

```{r}
# recount3 returns ENSEMBL IDs with version suffixes
# e.g. ENSG00000159111.16 — strip the version for compatibility

# Check what your gene IDs look like right now
cat("Sample gene IDs (raw):\n")
print(head(rownames(counts_colon), 5))
# Expected: ENSG00000278704.1, ENSG00000277400.1 ...

# Strip version suffix: ENSG00000159111.16 → ENSG00000159111
strip_version <- function(ids) sub("\\..*", "", ids)

rownames(counts_colon)       <- strip_version(rownames(counts_colon))
rownames(normal_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_coad_counts))
rownames(normal_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(normal_read_counts))
rownames(tumour_coad_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_coad_counts))
rownames(tumour_read_counts) <- strip_version(rownames(tumour_read_counts))

cat("\nAfter stripping versions:\n")
print(head(rownames(counts_colon), 5))
print(head(rownames(normal_coad_counts), 5))
print(head(rownames(tumour_read_counts), 5))
# Expected: ENSG00000278704, ENSG00000277400 ...

# LCP1 ENSEMBL ID
LCP1_ENSEMBL <- "ENSG00000159111"
cat("\nLCP1 (", LCP1_ENSEMBL, ") in GTEx colon:",
    LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(counts_colon), "\n")

# Find genes common across ALL five matrices
# (GTEx colon + COAD normal + READ normal + COAD tumour + READ tumour)
cat("\nFinding common genes across all sources...\n")
common_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    rownames(counts_colon),
    rownames(normal_coad_counts),
    rownames(normal_read_counts),
    rownames(tumour_coad_counts),
    rownames(tumour_read_counts)
))
cat("Common genes:", length(common_genes), "\n")

# Subset all matrices to common genes
counts_colon         <- counts_colon[common_genes, ]
normal_coad_counts   <- normal_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
normal_read_counts   <- normal_read_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_coad_counts   <- tumour_coad_counts[common_genes, ]
tumour_read_counts   <- tumour_read_counts[common_genes, ]

cat("LCP1 in common genes:", LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% common_genes, "\n")
cat("Block 2 complete ✓\n")
```

BLOCK 3 — BUILD COMBINED NORMAL REFERENCE MATRIX

```{r}
# Combine all three normal sources into one reference matrix
# GTEx colon + TCGA-COAD matched normal + TCGA-READ matched normal
#
# Biological justification: colon and rectal tissue share highly similar
# transcriptional profiles (Cancer Genome Atlas Research Network, 2012)
# Technical justification: all data processed through the same
# recount3 Monorail pipeline — minimises technical batch effects

normal_combined <- cbind(
    counts_colon,
    normal_coad_counts,
    normal_read_counts
)

# One batch label per sample — essential for ComBat-seq correction
n_colon <- ncol(counts_colon)
n_coad  <- ncol(normal_coad_counts)
n_read  <- ncol(normal_read_counts)

batch_labels <- c(
    rep("GTEx_Colon",       n_colon),
    rep("TCGA_COAD_Normal", n_coad),
    rep("TCGA_READ_Normal", n_read)
)

tissue_labels <- c(
    rep("colon_gtex",            n_colon),
    rep("colon_matched_normal",  n_coad),
    rep("rectum_matched_normal", n_read)
)

source_labels <- c(
    rep("GTEx", n_colon),
    rep("TCGA", n_coad + n_read)
)

cat("Combined normal matrix:", nrow(normal_combined),
    "genes ×", ncol(normal_combined), "samples\n")
cat("\nBatch distribution:\n")
print(table(batch_labels))
cat("\nTissue distribution:\n")
print(table(tissue_labels))
cat("Block 3 complete ✓\n")
```

**BLOCK 4 — QUALITY CONTROL**

```{r}
# ── Filter lowly expressed genes ──────────────────────────────────────
# Keep genes with >10 counts in at least 20% of all normal samples
# Genes expressed in fewer samples cannot support reliable
# co-expression estimates for WGCNA

cat("\nBLOCK 4: Quality Control\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep = "")

min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_combined) * 0.35)
keep_genes            <- rowSums(normal_combined > 10) >= min_samples_threshold

cat("Genes before filtering:", nrow(normal_combined), "\n")
cat("20% sample threshold:  ", min_samples_threshold, "samples\n")

normal_filtered <- normal_combined[keep_genes, ]
cat("Genes after filtering: ", nrow(normal_filtered), "\n")
cat("Genes removed:         ", sum(!keep_genes), "\n")

# Check LCP1 survived
lcp1_survived <- LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_filtered)
cat("\nLCP1 survived expression filter:", lcp1_survived, "\n")

if (!lcp1_survived) {
    # LCP1 is aberrantly expressed — low in normal colon is expected
    lcp1_expr_check <- normal_combined[LCP1_ENSEMBL, ]
    cat("LCP1 samples with >10 counts:",
        sum(lcp1_expr_check > 10), "of", ncol(normal_combined), "\n")
    cat("Lowering threshold to 10% to retain LCP1...\n")

    min_samples_threshold <- ceiling(ncol(normal_combined) * 0.10)
    keep_genes            <- rowSums(normal_combined > 10) >= min_samples_threshold
    normal_filtered       <- normal_combined[keep_genes, ]

    cat("Genes after 10% filter:", nrow(normal_filtered), "\n")
    cat("LCP1 now present:      ",
        LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_filtered), "\n")
}

# Library size check — remove very low quality samples
lib_sizes         <- colSums(normal_filtered)
low_lib_threshold <- quantile(lib_sizes, 0.05)
low_lib_flag      <- lib_sizes < low_lib_threshold

cat("\nLibrary size summary:\n")
print(summary(lib_sizes))

if (any(low_lib_flag)) {
    cat("\nRemoving", sum(low_lib_flag),
        "low-quality samples (below 5th percentile)\n")
    normal_filtered <- normal_filtered[, !low_lib_flag]
    batch_labels    <- batch_labels[!low_lib_flag]
    tissue_labels   <- tissue_labels[!low_lib_flag]
    source_labels   <- source_labels[!low_lib_flag]
    cat("Samples remaining:", ncol(normal_filtered), "\n")
} else {
    cat("All samples pass library size QC ✓\n")
}

cat("\nFinal normal reference after QC:\n")
cat("  Genes:       ", nrow(normal_filtered), "\n")
cat("  Samples:     ", ncol(normal_filtered), "\n")
cat("  LCP1 present:",
    LCP1_ENSEMBL %in% rownames(normal_filtered), "\n")
cat("Block 4 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# ── Diagnostic — run this before Block 5 ─────────────────────────────

cat("Checking corrected counts for problematic values...\n")

# Check for NA values
n_na <- sum(is.na(normal_filtered))
cat("NA values in normal_filtered:", n_na, "\n")

# Check for non-finite values (Inf, -Inf, NaN)
n_inf <- sum(!is.finite(normal_filtered))
cat("Non-finite values:", n_inf, "\n")

# Check for values exceeding integer range
int_max <- .Machine$integer.max  # 2,147,483,647
n_large <- sum(normal_filtered > int_max, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Values exceeding integer max:", n_large, "\n")

# Check for negative values (counts cannot be negative)
n_neg <- sum(normal_filtered < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Negative values:", n_neg, "\n")

# Show the range
cat("Count range: [",
    min(normal_filtered, na.rm = TRUE), ",",
    max(normal_filtered, na.rm = TRUE), "]\n")
```

Fix A — Values Too Large (Most Likely Cause)

```{r}
# ── Fix A: Clean normal_filtered → normal_filtered_clean ─────────────
# Problem: recount3 occasionally returns count values exceeding
# R's integer maximum (2,147,483,647) for highly expressed genes.
# Both ComBat-seq and DESeq2 require valid integer counts.
# Without this fix you get:
#   "NAs introduced by coercion to integer range"
#   "NA values are not allowed in the count matrix"
#
# Solution: cap oversized values, remove NAs, force integer storage
# Always applied to normal_filtered → produces normal_filtered_clean
# normal_filtered is never modified directly

cat("\nFix A: Cleaning raw counts before batch correction...\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep = "")

cat("Checking normal_filtered for problematic values...\n")
n_na    <- sum(is.na(normal_filtered))
n_inf   <- sum(!is.finite(normal_filtered))
n_large <- sum(normal_filtered > .Machine$integer.max, na.rm = TRUE)
n_neg   <- sum(normal_filtered < 0, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("  NA values:            ", n_na,    "\n")
cat("  Non-finite values:    ", n_inf,   "\n")
cat("  Values > integer max: ", n_large, "\n")
cat("  Negative values:      ", n_neg,   "\n")
cat("  Count range: [",
    min(normal_filtered, na.rm = TRUE), ",",
    max(normal_filtered, na.rm = TRUE), "]\n")

# Create cleaned copy
normal_filtered_clean <- normal_filtered

# 1. Cap values exceeding R integer maximum
normal_filtered_clean[
    normal_filtered_clean > .Machine$integer.max
] <- .Machine$integer.max

# 2. Replace NA and non-finite values with 0
normal_filtered_clean[!is.finite(normal_filtered_clean)] <- 0
normal_filtered_clean[is.na(normal_filtered_clean)]      <- 0

# 3. Remove negative values
normal_filtered_clean[normal_filtered_clean < 0] <- 0

# 4. Round and force integer storage mode
normal_filtered_clean <- round(normal_filtered_clean)
storage.mode(normal_filtered_clean) <- "integer"

# Verify
cat("\nAfter cleaning (normal_filtered_clean):\n")
cat("  NA values:            ",
    sum(is.na(normal_filtered_clean)),                           "\n")
cat("  Non-finite values:    ",
    sum(!is.finite(normal_filtered_clean)),                      "\n")
cat("  Values > integer max: ",
    sum(normal_filtered_clean > .Machine$integer.max),           "\n")
cat("  Negative values:      ",
    sum(normal_filtered_clean < 0),                             "\n")
cat("  Storage mode:         ",
    storage.mode(normal_filtered_clean),                         "\n")
cat("  Count range: [",
    min(normal_filtered_clean), ",",
    max(normal_filtered_clean), "]\n")

cat("Fix A complete ✓\n")
```

BLOCK 6 — BATCH CORRECTION

```{r}
# ── Batch correction with ComBat-seq ─────────────────────────────────
# Corrects systematic technical differences between:
#   GTEx_Colon       — GTEx portal processing pipeline
#   TCGA_COAD_Normal — TCGA/GDC processing pipeline
#   TCGA_READ_Normal — TCGA/GDC processing pipeline
#
# Input:  normal_filtered_clean (clean integer counts)
# Output: corrected_counts      (corrected integer counts)
#
# PCA before correction is generated here for comparison
# PCA after correction is generated in Block 5 after VST

cat("\nBLOCK 6: Batch Correction\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep = "")

# ── PCA before correction (visualisation only) ────────────────────────
# Quick VST on clean raw counts — for the before plot only
# NOT the final normalisation — Block 5 handles that

cat("Computing PCA before batch correction (visualisation only)...\n")

dds_vis <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = normal_filtered_clean,
    colData   = data.frame(
        batch  = factor(batch_labels),
        tissue = factor(tissue_labels),
        source = factor(source_labels),
        row.names = colnames(normal_filtered_clean)
    ),
    design = ~1
)
expr_vis   <- assay(vst(dds_vis, blind = TRUE))
pca_before <- prcomp(t(expr_vis), scale. = FALSE)
var_before <- pca_before$sdev^2 / sum(pca_before$sdev^2) * 100

pca_before_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_before$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_before$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_before <- ggplot(pca_before_df,
                   aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "PCA — before batch correction",
        subtitle = "GTEx and TCGA should cluster separately here (expected)",
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_before[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_before[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_before)
```

```{r}
# ── Apply ComBat-seq ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nApplying ComBat-seq...\n")
cat("Batches being corrected:\n")
print(table(batch_labels))

corrected_counts <- ComBat_seq(
    counts = normal_filtered_clean,
    batch  = batch_labels,
    group  = NULL
)

cat("Batch correction complete\n")
cat("Corrected counts:", nrow(corrected_counts), "genes ×",
    ncol(corrected_counts), "samples\n")

# ComBat-seq can occasionally reintroduce small negative values
n_neg_post <- sum(corrected_counts < 0, na.rm = TRUE)
n_na_post  <- sum(is.na(corrected_counts))

if (n_neg_post > 0 || n_na_post > 0) {
    cat("Minor clean-up on corrected_counts...\n")
    corrected_counts[is.na(corrected_counts)] <- 0
    corrected_counts[corrected_counts < 0]    <- 0
    corrected_counts                          <- round(corrected_counts)
    storage.mode(corrected_counts)            <- "integer"
    cat("  Negatives removed:", n_neg_post, "\n")
    cat("  NAs removed:      ", n_na_post,  "\n")
} else {
    cat("corrected_counts is clean ✓\n")
}

cat("Block 6 complete ✓\n")
```

BLOCK 5 — VST NORMALISATION

```{r}
# ── VST Normalisation ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Applied to corrected_counts — the ComBat-seq output from Block 6
# Variance Stabilising Transformation (VST) via DESeq2:
#   - Stabilises variance across the expression range
#   - Produces continuous values suitable for Pearson correlation
#   - Required by WGCNA before network construction

cat("\nBLOCK 5: VST Normalisation\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep = "")

col_data_normal <- data.frame(
    batch   = factor(batch_labels),
    tissue  = factor(tissue_labels),
    source  = factor(source_labels),
    row.names = colnames(corrected_counts)
)

cat("Creating DESeq2 object from corrected counts...\n")
dds_corrected <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = corrected_counts,
    colData   = col_data_normal,
    design    = ~1
)

cat("Applying VST...\n")
vst_corrected  <- vst(dds_corrected, blind = TRUE)
expr_corrected <- assay(vst_corrected)

cat("VST complete ✓\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(expr_corrected), "genes ×",
    ncol(expr_corrected), "samples\n")
cat("VST value range: [",
    round(min(expr_corrected), 2), ",",
    round(max(expr_corrected), 2), "]\n")
# Expected: continuous floats between roughly 4 and 20
```

```{r}
# ── PCA after correction ──────────────────────────────────────────────
pca_after  <- prcomp(t(expr_corrected), scale. = FALSE)
var_after  <- pca_after$sdev^2 / sum(pca_after$sdev^2) * 100

pca_after_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_after$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_after$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_after <- ggplot(pca_after_df,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "PCA — after batch correction",
        subtitle = "Batches should now intermingle — biological variation remains",
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_after[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_after[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_after)

# Save both PCA plots side by side for thesis Methods figure
pdf("plots/batch_correction_PCA.pdf", width = 16, height = 7)
grid.arrange(p_before, p_after, ncol = 2)
dev.off()
cat("PCA comparison saved to plots/batch_correction_PCA.pdf\n")

cat("\nCHECK THE PCA PLOTS:\n")
cat("  BEFORE: GTEx and TCGA should cluster separately\n")
cat("  AFTER:  batches should intermingle\n")
cat("  If still fully separate, consult supervisor before proceeding\n")
cat("Block 5 complete ✓\n")
```

```{r}
# Cap values at integer maximum before DESeq2
# This affects a tiny fraction of very highly expressed genes
# and has negligible impact on WGCNA results

normal_filtered_clean_1 <- normal_combined_1

# Replace values above integer max with the integer max
normal_filtered_clean_1[normal_filtered_clean_1 > .Machine$integer.max] <-
    .Machine$integer.max

# Replace any NA or non-finite values with 0
normal_filtered_clean_1[!is.finite(normal_filtered_clean_1)] <- 0
normal_filtered_clean_1[is.na(normal_filtered_clean_1)]      <- 0

# Ensure all values are non-negative
normal_filtered_clean_1[normal_filtered_clean_1 < 0] <- 0

# Verify
cat("After cleaning:\n")
cat("  NA values:     ", sum(is.na(normal_filtered_clean_1)), "\n")
cat("  Non-finite:    ", sum(!is.finite(normal_filtered_clean_1)), "\n")
cat("  Values > max:  ",
    sum(normal_filtered_clean_1 > .Machine$integer.max), "\n")
cat("  Negative:      ",
    sum(normal_filtered_clean_1 < 0), "\n")
cat("  Range: [",
    min(normal_filtered_clean_1), ",",
    max(normal_filtered_clean_1), "]\n")

# Convert to integer matrix explicitly
normal_filtered_clean_1 <- round(normal_filtered_clean_1)
storage.mode(normal_filtered_clean_1) <- "integer"
cat("Storage mode:", storage.mode(normal_filtered_clean_1), "\n")
```

LIMMA BATCH CORRECTION

```{r}
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# BLOCK 6 — BATCH CORRECTION via limma (replacing ComBat-seq)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Rationale for switching from ComBat-seq to limma:
#
# 1. ComBat-seq group argument is unavailable — batch and tissue
#    are perfectly confounded in this dataset
# 2. limma::removeBatchEffect handles size-imbalanced batches
#    (700 GTEx vs 51 TCGA) more gracefully than ComBat-seq
# 3. limma works on VST-normalised values — no integer overflow risk
# 4. Widely used in published WGCNA pipelines
#
# Approach: VST on clean raw counts → limma batch correction
# expr_corrected is the final output — used directly in Block 7

cat("\nBLOCK 6: Batch Correction via limma\n")
cat(rep("-", 42), "\n", sep="")

library(limma)

# ── Step 1: VST on clean raw counts ──────────────────────────────────
# This also serves as the normalisation (Block 5 is merged here)
# VST first, then remove batch effect linearly

cat("Step 1: Applying VST to clean raw counts...\n")

col_data_normal <- data.frame(
    batch   = factor(batch_labels),
    tissue  = factor(tissue_labels),
    source  = factor(source_labels),
    row.names = colnames(normal_filtered_clean_1)
)

dds_for_limma <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
    countData = normal_filtered_clean_1,
    colData   = col_data_normal,
    design    = ~1
)

vst_for_limma  <- vst(dds_for_limma, blind = TRUE)
expr_vst_limma <- assay(vst_for_limma)

cat("VST complete\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(expr_vst_limma), "genes ×",
    ncol(expr_vst_limma), "samples\n")

# ── PCA before correction ─────────────────────────────────────────────
pca_before <- prcomp(t(expr_vst_limma), scale. = FALSE)
var_before <- pca_before$sdev^2 / sum(pca_before$sdev^2) * 100

pca_before_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_before$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_before$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_before <- ggplot(pca_before_df,
                   aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "PCA — before batch correction",
        subtitle = "GTEx and TCGA should cluster separately here (expected)",
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_before[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_before[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_before)

# ── Step 2: limma batch correction ───────────────────────────────────
# removeBatchEffect removes linear batch effects from VST values
# It does NOT require group — handles confounding differently
# by projecting out the batch component directly

cat("\nStep 2: Applying limma batch correction...\n")
cat("Batches:", paste(unique(batch_labels), collapse = ", "), "\n")

expr_corrected_limma <- removeBatchEffect(
    expr_vst_limma,
    batch = batch_labels
)

cat("limma correction complete\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(expr_corrected_limma), "×",
    ncol(expr_corrected_limma), "\n")
cat("Value range: [",
    round(min(expr_corrected_limma), 2), ",",
    round(max(expr_corrected_limma), 2), "]\n")

# ── PCA after correction ──────────────────────────────────────────────
pca_after  <- prcomp(t(expr_corrected_limma), scale. = FALSE)
var_after  <- pca_after$sdev^2 / sum(pca_after$sdev^2) * 100

pca_after_df <- data.frame(
    PC1    = pca_after$x[, 1],
    PC2    = pca_after$x[, 2],
    batch  = batch_labels,
    tissue = tissue_labels,
    source = source_labels
)

p_after <- ggplot(pca_after_df,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, colour = batch, shape = source)) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
    labs(
        title    = "PCA — after limma batch correction",
        subtitle = "Batches should now intermingle — biological variation remains",
        x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_after[1], 1), "% variance)"),
        y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_after[2], 1), "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

print(p_after)

# Save both PCA plots
pdf("plots/batch_correction_PCA.pdf", width = 16, height = 7)
grid.arrange(p_before, p_after, ncol = 2)
dev.off()
cat("PCA plots saved to plots/batch_correction_PCA.pdf\n")

# ── Residual batch effect diagnostic ─────────────────────────────────
batch_numeric  <- as.numeric(factor(batch_labels))
pc1_batch_cor  <- cor(pca_after$x[, 1], batch_numeric)
pc2_batch_cor  <- cor(pca_after$x[, 2], batch_numeric)

cat("\nResidual batch correlation with PCs:\n")
cat("  PC1 vs batch:", round(pc1_batch_cor, 3), "\n")
cat("  PC2 vs batch:", round(pc2_batch_cor, 3), "\n")
cat("  Target: |r| < 0.2 for adequate correction\n")

if (abs(pc1_batch_cor) > 0.3) {
    cat("\n  WARNING: PC1 still correlated with batch (r > 0.3)\n")
    cat("  This may reflect true biological differences\n")
    cat("  between GTEx and TCGA tissue collection methods\n")
    cat("  Proceed to WGCNA — the network will capture shared patterns\n")
} else {
    cat("  Batch correction adequate ✓\n")
}

cat("\nCHECK THE PCA PLOTS:\n")
cat("  BEFORE: GTEx and TCGA should cluster separately\n")
cat("  AFTER:  batches should intermingle\n")
cat("  PC1 variance should drop from ~43% toward ~15-30%\n")
cat("Block 6 complete ✓\n")
```
